To find the values of $ a $ and $ b $ that satisfy the condition that the surface area of the given parametric surface is exactly 1000 square units, we start by computing the surface area of the parametric surface defined by the equations:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v), \quad y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v), \quad z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

where $ 0 \leq u \leq \pi $ and $ 0 \leq v < 2\pi $.

---

### Step 1: Compute the Surface Area

The surface area $ A $ of a parametric surface given by $ \vec{r}(u, v) = x(u, v)\hat{i} + y(u, v)\hat{j} + z(u, v)\hat{k} $ is given by:

$$
A = \iint_D \left\| \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} \right\| \, du \, dv
$$

First, compute the partial derivatives:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Compute the cross product:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Expanding the determinant:

$$
\vec{r}_u \times \vec{r}_v = \left( a^2 \sin(u) \cos^2(v) + a b \sin(u) \
